riddles.riddle: I speak without a mouth and hear without ears. I have nobody, but I come alive with the wind. What am I?
The more of me you take, the more you leave behind. What am I?
I’m tall when I’m young, and I’m short when I’m old. What am I?
I have keys but no locks. I have space but no room. You can enter, but you can’t go outside. What am I?
I am always hungry and will die if not fed, but whatever I touch will soon turn red. What am I?
I can fly without wings. I can cry without eyes. Wherever I go, darkness flies. What am I?
I am taken from a mine, and shut up in a wooden case, from which I am never released, yet I am used by almost every person. What am I?
The more you take from me, the bigger I get. What am I?
I have cities but no houses, forests but no trees, and rivers but no water. What am I?
I’m often running, yet I have no legs. You can enter me, but you can’t go outside. What am I?
I can be cracked, made, told, and played. What am I?
I am not alive, but I grow; I don’t have lungs, but I need air; I don’t have a mouth, but water kills me. What am I?
The person who makes it, sells it. The person who buys it never uses it. The person who uses it never knows they are using it. What is it?
I am always in front of you but can never be seen. What am I?
I can bring back the dead; make us cry, make us laugh, make us young; I am born in an instant but last a lifetime. What am I?
I turn once, what is out will not get in. I turn again, what is in will not get out. What am I?
I can be long or short; I can be grown or bought; I can be painted or left bare; I can be round or square. What am I?
I have lakes with no water, mountains with no stone, and cities with no buildings. What am I?
I fly without wings. I cry without eyes. Wherever I go, darkness flies. What am I?
I go in hard, come out soft, and am never the same. What am I?
